title: Identity Verification & MFA
description: Verifying user identity through multiple factors before granting access — the foundation of Zero Trust.
fullExplanation: Identity is the new perimeter in Zero Trust. Every access request must be authenticated and authorized regardless of network location. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) requires something you know (password), something you have (phone/token), and/or something you are (biometric). Modern identity platforms use risk-based adaptive authentication — requiring stronger verification when risk signals are elevated (new device, unusual location, sensitive resource).

mfaTypes.phishable: Yes — can be phished in real-time

keyFacts: FIDO2/WebAuthn: phishing-resistant MFA — key bound to specific domain, can't be replayed
MFA fatigue attack: flood user with push notifications until they approve — use number matching
Conditional Access: require MFA only when risk is elevated (new device, unusual location)
SSO (Single Sign-On): authenticate once, access many apps — reduces password fatigue
PAM (Privileged Access Management): extra controls for admin accounts — session recording, JIT access
Passwordless: FIDO2 + biometric replaces password entirely
NIST SP 800-63B: US government MFA guidelines — recommends against SMS OTP
